notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Orion, which the field rarely builds (1.2% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Gadget (it never builds Azure Core, Entropy) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 11 (Orion), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +13.7% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 2,955 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 5% of our best core on magical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 2,500 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 1% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 2,955 effective HP vs magical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 5% of our best core on magical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 26% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 26% less hard, and this kit gives up 26% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 26% less hard, and this kit gives up 26% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 26% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 26% less hard, and this kit gives up 26% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their burst combo hits 21% less hard, and this kit gives up 26% of its damage for it.
